baseline.final_answer: A fixed-wing plane is an aircraft that derives its lift from the forward motion of its wings, as opposed to rotary-wing aircraft like helicopters. Examples include surveying planes and drones like quad-copters and fixed-wing planes used for long-distance monitoring.
